ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass. The depicted figures are generic armored Orzhov knights; the Hipsters of the Coast preview confirms the art brief was for an unnamed 'Orzhov knight' tasked with collecting debts. No named character identification in any source consulted.

trivia_bullets.text: Charity Extractor is a War of the Spark common (set released 2019-05-03), the third Ravnica-focused set in the block and the climax of the Nicol Bolas storyline; its flavor sits with the Orzhov Syndicate, the guild known on Ravnica for tithe and debt collection. [Scryfall] [MTG Wiki: War of the Spark]
sources: MTG Wiki: War of the Spark

trivia_bullets.text: Art by Matt Stewart, oil on Ampersand gessoboard at 16" x 20". Stewart's preview commentary notes the art brief specified a generic 'Orzhov knight' demanding payment from debtors; Stewart added the keys around the knight's neck and the lockbox at the figure's side as his own choices to underline the templar's seriousness about collection. [Hipsters of the Coast: 'Behind the Front Lines' preview, 2019-04]
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Behind the Front Lines' preview (2019-04)

trivia_bullets.text: Card flavor text reads verbatim: "War or no war, your donations are due, as always." — no in-universe speaker is attributed on the printed card; the line is a guild-voice quip framing Orzhov tithe-collection as indifferent to the planeswalker war raging on Ravnica. [Scryfall]

{3}{B} 1/5 Human Knight with lifelink, common — a defensive grinder rather than an aggressive body. EDHREC tracks it in roughly 0.01% of decks (~585 of ~4.47M); its most common Commander shells lean on its high-toughness statline (Doran, Besieged by Time; Doran, the Siege Tower) or knight-tribal (Syr Gwyn, Hero of Ashvale). It sees no Standard play and was never reprinted. [EDHREC: Charity Extractor] [Scryfall]

Functionally and flavor-wise it sits next to other Orzhov 'collector' cards in WAR — most directly Tithebearer Giant (WAR #107), another debt-collection creature in the same set — supporting the read that WAR's Orzhov slot is doing a deliberate 'the church still bills you mid-apocalypse' beat rather than centering any specific named NPC. [Hipsters of the Coast: 'Behind the Front Lines' preview, 2019-04] [Scryfall set page: War of the Spark]
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Behind the Front Lines' preview (2019-04)

related_cards.name: Tithebearer Giant
related_cards.note: Sibling Orzhov-flavored debt-collection creature in the same set (WAR #107); explicitly grouped with Charity Extractor in the Hipsters preview as part of WAR's Orzhov 'collections' slot.

related_cards.name: Tithe Drinker
related_cards.note: Earlier Orzhov common ({W}{B}, lifelink + extort) embodying the same guild trope of vampires/knights as tithe collectors; common touchstone for the Orzhov-tithe flavor archetype.

related_cards.name: Syr Gwyn, Hero of Ashvale
related_cards.note: One of the more common Commander shells that runs Charity Extractor on EDHREC; knight-tribal payoff that brings the card into a deck for its creature type rather than its lifelink.

related_cards.name: Doran, the Siege Tower
related_cards.note: EDHREC-cited commander shell where Charity Extractor's 1/5 statline (toughness > power) becomes an actual offensive threat under Doran's static ability.

related_cards.name: Kaya, Orzhov Usurper
related_cards.note: The Orzhov guildmaster during the War of the Spark arc; relevant lore context for any card carrying Orzhov tithe-collection flavor in this set, per MTG Wiki: War of the Spark.
